perItemFindings.label: def:global-feasible-fiber-decision
perItemFindings.one_line: The core includes real-QE/CAD decision of the feasibility formula, but the plan deliberately defines only an existential Prop and supplies neither a CAD gate nor a conditional interface for the absent decision engine.

perItemFindings.label: def:direction-selector
perItemFindings.one_line: Its promised globally finite semialgebraic selector depends on the same absent QE/CAD engine; an Option-valued Prop definition without evaluability does not realize that construction.

perItemFindings.label: def:real-atlas-handle
perItemFindings.one_line: The plan retains only certificate/set fragments although this core handle explicitly runs CAD; it silently omits the research-scale real-closed-field decision substrate rather than gating it.

perItemFindings.label: thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery
perItemFindings.one_line: The notes call this a deferred >=3-file apolarity/catalecticant plus Zariski-locus project, yet defer_tier is false and no named external-theory conditional is planned.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:generic-separation
perItemFindings.one_line: This node is likewise declared DEFER because it requires the absent I-1/I-2 apparatus, but is marked build-inline rather than honestly routed as a sized external dependency.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:minimal-real-information-order
perItemFindings.one_line: Its claimed proof needs both absent apolarity infrastructure and the truncated Hamburger moment problem (I-4), while defer_tier is false.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:generic-exceptional-locus
perItemFindings.one_line: I-1/I-2 are acknowledged as absent research-scale algebraic-geometry infrastructure, but the deferred theorem is not converted to an explicit conditional/gate.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:real-exceptional-atlas
perItemFindings.one_line: The plan correctly identifies CAD/QE and the full truncated moment problem as absent; Causalean's MomentSliceSupport is only a two-moment support result, not a total fit, so these must be named external conditional substrate rather than deferred theorem debt.

review.verbatim_critique: The Causalean/Mathlib reuse decisions themselves are sound: no cited declaration is being used as a mismatched theorem, and the PO/SCM bypass is appropriate for this binary-form LvLiNGAM world. Re-plan the CAD-facing definitions and all I-1/I-2/I-4-dependent cruxes: classify the missing named theories as research-scale external, gate/thread only the irreducible external facts (without bundling local reductions), and mark the presently declared deferred cruxes honestly. Do not present Prop-only feasibility or a classical partial definition as the core's finite semialgebraic decision procedure.

perItemFindings.label: thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery
perItemFindings.one_line: I-1 apolarity/catalecticant machinery and I-2 Zariski/real-open theory are named, absent whole theories—not a bounded handful of lemmas—yet the plan leaves the headline as a deferred theorem rather than threading named external assumptions.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:generic-separation
perItemFindings.one_line: Its essential apolar certificate (I-1) is research-scale absent substrate; a deferred crux does not satisfy the required external-theory routing, and its conditional result must expose that assumption to consumers.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:generic-exceptional-locus
perItemFindings.one_line: The proposed proof requires absent image-variety dimension/codimension and apolarity apparatus (I-1/I-2), explicitly described as multi-file external theory, so it must be conditional on named substrate rather than merely deferred.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:minimal-real-information-order
perItemFindings.one_line: The full truncated Hamburger moment problem (I-4), alongside apolarity (I-1), is an absent named theory; reclassify only those irreducible facts as assumptions and thread them, including through the explicit oeq:generic-separation dependency.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:real-exceptional-atlas
perItemFindings.one_line: RealExceptionalAtlas assumes the entire paper-specific finite-atlas conclusion, fusing CAD/QE and truncated-Hamburger external facts with derivable feasibility/incidence/elimination reductions; the gate must contain only separate irreducible external primitives.

perItemFindings.label: def:global-feasible-fiber-decision / def:direction-selector / def:real-atlas-handle
perItemFindings.one_line: The core promises QE-based exact feasibility and an evaluable finite semialgebraic selector, but the plan retains only relational Props while declaring the CAD and moment substrate standalone; the conditional evaluability/exact-feasibility claims need explicitly threaded named interfaces.

review.verbatim_critique: Size I-1, I-2, and I-4 as research-scale external theories and replace the affected deferred headlines by conditional theorem assemblies over narrowly stated named assumptions; thread those assumptions through every dependent result. Split RealExceptionalAtlas into at least a generic real-QE/CAD correctness interface and a truncated-Hamburger atomic-representation interface, then prove/plan the project-specific feasibility, incidence, and atlas reductions separately. Thread the QE interface to every claim of finite/evaluable selection and the moment interface to every claimed equivalence with real source feasibility.

perItemFindings.label: S1
perItemFindings.one_line: The trace incorrectly says Causalean has no non-Gaussian source/mixing artifact: `Causalean.Discovery.LiNGAM.Kurtosis.cross_fourth_cumulant_eq_sum` models independent centered finite-fourth-moment sources and linear mixtures. It is not a total fit for overcomplete all-order LvLiNGAM, but the bypass must explicitly survey and reject it on that basis.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:real-exceptional-atlas
perItemFindings.one_line: The core node is the derived finite-atlas conclusion, but its planned `lean_kind:"assumption"` is only the conjunction of external I-3 QE/CAD and I-4 truncated-Hamburger interfaces; those facts do not themselves realize the core conclusion. Keep I-3/I-4 as separate external assumptions and plan the atlas as their conditional derived theorem.

perItemFindings.label: thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery / oeq:generic-separation / oeq:generic-exceptional-locus / oeq:minimal-real-information-order
perItemFindings.one_line: I-1 and I-2 are named research-scale external prerequisites only in notes, not explicit assumed interfaces in the consuming declarations' `hyps`; calling this a P4 constraint does not thread the conditional dependencies.

review.verbatim_critique: Update S1's substrate survey with the existing LiNGAM kurtosis module and record its concrete abstraction mismatch. Split I-3 and I-4 into separate external interface assumptions, derive the real-atlas conclusion conditionally from them, and represent I-1/I-2 as explicit external assumptions threaded through every deferred theorem that uses them; do not use a core-derived theorem node as a bare proxy for its substrate.

reason: Case 1 (parent-abstraction check RULED OUT user) + regression (a) same-flag-repeating: all three reviews agree the Causalean/Mathlib reuse and PO/SCM bypass are sound and the math/core is correct — the defect is purely the PLAN's mishandling of research-scale external theories (I-1 apolarity/catalecticant, I-2 Zariski dimension, I-3 QE/CAD, I-4 truncated-Hamburger). Fix is not 'invent substrate' (→ not user) nor a math re-derive (→ not stage_0): the reviewers prescribe a concrete plan restructuring — thread the absent theories as NAMED external conditional interfaces and split the bundled RealExceptionalAtlas gate. The producer is stuck re-firing the same 'dispatch background builders' substrate-build checkpoint instead of re-planning the flagged nodes. Matches the plan_gate per-boundary heuristic.
proposed_action: Re-plan the flagged nodes in formalization.md: (1) Split oeq:real-exceptional-atlas's single bundled `lean_kind:"assumption"` gate into TWO separate named external interfaces — I-3 (generic real-QE/CAD decision correctness) and I-4 (truncated-Hamburger Hankel-PSD atomic representation) — and re-plan the atlas as a conditional DERIVED theorem over them, keeping the project-specific feasibility/incidence/elimination reductions as local derivations (not fused into the gate). (2) Thread I-1 (apolarity/catalecticant) and I-2 (Zariski/real-open dimension) as EXPLICIT external assumption interfaces in the `hyps` of every consuming deferred decl (thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery, oeq:generic-separation, oeq:generic-exceptional-locus, oeq:minimal-real-information-order), and set defer_tier/external-conditional honestly rather than build-inline. (3) Replace the Prop-only feasibility/selector defs (def:global-feasible-fiber-decision, def:direction-selector, def:real-atlas-handle) with the QE-interface-threaded conditional-evaluability form so the finite semialgebraic decision is gated on I-3, not silently claimed. (4) Update S1's substrate survey to survey-and-reject Causalean.Discovery.LiNGAM.Kurtosis.cross_fourth_cumulant_eq_sum with its concrete abstraction mismatch (two-moment/finite-order vs overcomplete all-order LvLiNGAM), recording it as bypass-justified. Change no .tex math.
